div.h2: Technical Specification: Compile → Verify → Load → Attach → Execute

ol.li: Safe by Design: The verifier performs static analysis. If memory safety cannot be mathematically proven, the program is never loaded.
Efficiency: JIT compilation converts generic BPF instructions into native opcodes (MOV, JMP, etc.) for zero-latency execution.
Isolation: Programs cannot access arbitrary kernel memory; they only see the context passed by the hook.
